programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team revive a powered football wheelchair, some meticulously handcrafted jewellery, a bagatelle board, and a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: When a killer gang use a secret language to discuss an attack, police turn to a forensic linguist. And a huge cannabis operation is brought down with the help of a drugs expert.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel meets a man in Carnoustie renovating a home for him, his dog and a menagerie of other animals to live in. Dion decides it's not safe for him to go into a house in Merseyside.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Carmarthenshire, where experts Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson advise the reds and blue teams as they battle it out in Llandeilo.

programme.title: BBC News at One including BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Hebden Bridge, friends Emma and Sophie battle to raise money for Dove House, gambling on vintage fashion, retro collectibles and a bold sleeping-bag upcycle.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team are called in to give the Puffer Pub a makeover on the tiny island of Easdale. Can they complete the work ahead of the World Stone Skimming Championships.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from the city of Wolverhampton to find a new home and lifestyle in Shropshire, before braving an outdoors adventure playground with a giant zip wire.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Tiana's work is packed with character, but can she price it to sell? Ceramic artist Pip has the skills to succeed, but can her mentor instil the self-confidence and belief she needs.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz where contestants try to score as few points as possible by coming up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast by the Liberal Democrats
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Liberal Democrats for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki starts to worry about the family, Max and Cindy grow closer, and the Mitchells rally together.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Suzie fixes a football from a Scottish Cup final, Chris repairs a magazine from a Japanese POW camp, Geoff restores a pachinko machine, and Rebecca fixes a beautiful Cretan lyra.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more amateurs work to secure a place in this week's quarter-final as they are put through their paces by Grace and Anna in the second heat of the week.

programme.title: BBC News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth II: Her Story, Our Century
programme.desc: A century of change in Britain through the prism of Queen Elizabeth II's remarkable life.

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity special of the high-stakes quiz, featuring dancer Amy Dowden, actor Nina Wadia, Gladiator Montell Douglas and actress and musician Toyah Willcox.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Untying the knot in a dressing gown cord used to murder a woman finally nails her killer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross meets an avid collector of retro bikes, and the teams hunt for bargains in Derbyshire with experts Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer.

programme.title: BBC News at One including BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Aberystwyth, confidence clashes with caution as mother and son contestants hunt student town treasures, gamble on creative upcycles and face a tense live-streamed auction.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team have been tasked with making over an apothecary on Harris. The large, cavernous space will need completely renovating on a tiny budget.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a south east London couple find a family home on the Norfolk-Suffolk border, with homes that include two listed gems and a striking mystery house.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Can pyrographer Richard make a full-time living from his craft, and will Connie be able to build the embroidery business she dreams of to replace her academic career.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz where contestants try to score as few points as possible by coming up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast by the Conservative Party
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Conservative Party for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max and Cindy share a moment, Ravi finally gets the help he needs, Lauren and Mark team up, and Sam gives Mark an idea.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: With over 4,500km behind them, the race steps up a gear as the remaining teams learn the fourth checkpoint lies in Georgia's futuristic capital, Tbilisi.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's the first quarter-final, where this week's six most talented amateurs gather to face off against each other for the first time.

programme.title: BBC News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Lighthearted discussion of the day's news.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Rhod Gilbert, Jill Scott, Aisling Bea, Guz Khan, Nitro and the Rev Kate Bottley try to fill in the missing blanks and win prizes for some lucky contestants.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.
